entry.title: Lehar / Parlour Paper
entry.summary: The query raises an important observation regarding the presence of empty blocks in blockchain technology, occurring minutes or more after a previous block. This phenomenon directly aligns with the null hypothesis, suggesting that there might be underlying mechanics at play that are not immediately obvious. The inquiry seeks a straightforward explanation for why these empty blocks appear within the blockchain context, pointing towards a need for clarity on the processes and factors that contribute to this occurrence. Understanding the reasons behind the formation of empty blocks is crucial for comprehending the overall functionality and efficiency of blockchain systems.
